title: Problem Solving – Find the Whole Group Using Unit Fractions
essential_question: How can you find the total number in a group when you know a unit fraction of the group?
strategies: if 1/n of the group = a known number, multiply that number by n to find the whole

pretest.question: 1/3 of a group is 4. How many are in the whole group?
1/2 of a group is 5. How many are in the whole group?
1/4 of a number is 3. What is the number?
Lily has some stickers. 1/6 of her stickers is 5. How many stickers does she have in all?
1/8 of the marbles in a bag is 3. How many marbles are in the bag?

learn.title: From Part to Whole
learn.content: If you know a unit fraction of a group, you can find the whole group by multiplying. Example: 1/4 of a group is 6. The whole group = 6 × 4 = 24.

learn.title: Why Multiply?
learn.content: A unit fraction like 1/4 means the whole is made of 4 equal parts. If 1 part = 6, then 4 parts = 6 + 6 + 6 + 6 = 24. Multiplying is a shortcut: 6 × 4 = 24.

learn.title: Check Your Answer
learn.content: After finding the whole, check by dividing. If the whole is 24 and the fraction is 1/4, then 24 ÷ 4 = 6. That matches the given part, so the answer is correct.
